entry.title: Exploring Various Approaches to Managing Carryover Functionality in SF Time Off
entry.content:
In most countries, it is common for organizations to offer employees the option to carry over their leave balance. However, managing the carry over functionality within SuccessFactors can sometimes be confusing. The question arises whether to extend the bookable period in the Time Account Type or create a complex interim rule to address the requirement.

To help navigate this challenge, this post delves into the different approaches that can be taken to manage the carry over functionality in SF Time Off.

"Images/data in this post are from a demo system. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental."

Scenario 1:

One approach is to create a separate time account specifically for carryover leave. This approach enables organizations to manage the amount of leave that can be carried over and allows employees to book their carryover leave separately.

One common requirement is to move a limited number of days or the entire balance to the next account while allowing employees to use it during the next cycle. In such cases, a straightforward approach is to create a PEP (Period End Processing) rule to deduct the days from the current account balance and post them in the new account balance.

However, it is important to note that this approach may not be suitable for all organizations/ countries. For example, if an organization has a policy that limits the duration of the bookable period of the carried over leaves, a different approach may be necessary.

Scenario 2:

Another approach is to extend the bookable period in the Time Account Type. This approach allows employees to book their carryover leave within the same time account. However, this approach may not be feasible if the organization has a policy that limits the amount of leave that can be carried over.

In Denmark, for example, the annual leave cycle runs from September to August, but employees are allowed to book their leave until December. In January, any remaining balance is carried forward to the next year's account balance, up to a maximum of 5 days. In this post, we will explore a solution to manage this requirement.

The first step in managing this requirement is to extend the Account Booking Offset (Months) to 4. This will allow employees to use their entire balance to book their holidays until December. By extending the Account Offset, organizations can ensure that employees have access to the full balance of their annual leave.

The second step is to create a PEP (Period End Processing) Rule to run at the end of the bookable period to move any remaining balance to the next account year balance. This PEP Rule will ensure that any balance up to 5 days is carried forward to the next year's account balance, as required by law.

Scenario 3:

A third approach is to create a convoluted interim rule to manage the carryover functionality. This approach allows organizations to customize the rules based on their specific requirements. However, it can be time-consuming and complicated to manage, especially if there are frequent changes to the rules.

In China, for example, the Annual Leave cycle runs from January to December, and employees are allowed to carry forward a maximum of 5 days of leave from the previous balance. However, they can only use these days until March of the following year.

To accommodate this requirement, organizations need to create multiple interim rules. The first step is to create an Interim Account Update rule for the Annual Leave time account type to deduct the balance. This rule will ensure that any balance carried over from the previous year is deducted from the current year's Annual Leave time account.

Next, organizations need to create an Adhoc Time Account Type to store the balance. This account type will be used to store the limited balance from the previous year's Annual Leave time account.

The third step is to create an Interim Account Update rule to post the balance to the Adhoc time Account type. This rule will ensure that the limited balance from the previous year's Annual Leave time account is posted to the Adhoc time Account type.

Finally, account creation and Interim Account update are all manual processes. Organizations must create the accounts and update the balances manually.

Refer KBA for detailed steps - https://me.sap.com/notes/2530361

Summary:

In conclusion, managing the carryover functionality in SF Time Off can be challenging, but there are different approaches that organizations can take to manage it effectively. By carefully considering the advantages and disadvantages of each approach, organizations can select the most suitable option that aligns with their specific HR policies as well as ensure compliance with legal requirements.

entry.title: Creating a SuccessFactors Learning Story Report: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Creating a SuccessFactors Learning Story Report: A Comprehensive Guide

In the realm of learning and development, data-driven insights are pivotal for optimizing training programs and enhancing employee growth. SuccessFactors Learning, a leading learning management system (LMS) by SAP, provides robust reporting tools to help organizations make informed decisions based on their training data. However, transforming this data into a compelling report story requires more than just generating charts and tables; it involves crafting a narrative that communicates the value and impact of your learning initiatives.

In this blog, we’ll walk you through the steps to build a meaningful story report using SuccessFactors Learning Data Sets. From setting objectives to presenting findings, these steps will help you create reports that drive strategic decisions and foster continuous improvement.

Step 1: Define Your Report Objectives

Clarify Your Goals: Before diving into the data, determine the specific goals of your report. Are you looking to evaluate the effectiveness of a training program, assess learner engagement, or monitor compliance with mandatory training? Clearly defined objectives will guide your data collection and analysis process.

Examples of Objectives:

  • Measure the impact of a new training program on employee performance.
  • Track completion rates and compliance for required certifications.
  • Identify trends in learner engagement across different departments.

Step 2: Collect and Prepare Data

Identify Key Metrics: SuccessFactors Learning offers a variety of data points. Select metrics that align with your objectives. Key metrics might include:

  • Course Completion Rates: Percentage of employees who complete training programs.
  • Learner Engagement: Metrics such as login frequency, time spent on courses, and interaction with content.
  • Assessment Scores: Results from quizzes and exams to evaluate learning outcomes.
  • Compliance Metrics: Data on completion of mandatory training and certifications.

Step 4: Visualize the Data

Effective Visuals: Use Tables, charts, graphs, and dashboards to make your report more engaging and easier to understand. SuccessFactors Learning provides various visualization tools to help you present your data clearly.

 Step by Step guide on how to report story using SuccessFactors Learning Dataset

Prerequisites

  • Enable Story Report
  • Learning Administrators access
  • Enable Create Story and Share Reports to Groups & Roles permissions.
  • Enable the Create Story permission, and select the schemas you want to use while creating report stories.
  • Enable schema-specific permissions to access data from the schema tables. Refer to the Schema Permissions Required for Using Story topic for more information.

Access the Admin Center

  • Log in to your SuccessFactors account with administrative rights.
  • Navigate to the Admin Center.

Go to Reporting

  • In the Admin Center, locate the Reporting section.
  • Click on Reporting to create new report and view & edit existing created and assigned reports.

 Creating Query

  • In the Reporting section,  Click on the New to create new report.

  • Choose the Story, from the type of report you want to create.

  • System will prompt option to choose the Connection ( SAPSFSFREP for BizX modules Reporting & SAPSFSFLMS for Learning Reporting)
  • Choose the SAPSFSFLMS connection for creating learning reporting.

 Note: Currently, it’s not feasible to create report using blended data (BizX and LMS data).

 

  • You can see the available LMS Data Sets from the right side of panel.
  • You can choose data sets to build your query as per the data required to build the dashboard.

 You can now save the query and click on OK to jump into the story designer page

 

 

Creating dashboard

  • Choose the Page Layout and Apply (You can select predefined template or can create a blank page).

  • Select “Canvas” from the type of page you want to create

Types of Pages: The Canvas page is the default page of your Story report. It's a flexible space where you can explore and present your data. You can use Canvas pages to bring your story to life. Add charts, tables, or some other objects that make your data visually appealing.

The Responsive page lets you create lanes to section the page content into groups. By default, a Responsive page starts with two lanes and you can add tiles (widgets) to either lane. Tiles within a lane stay together when the Responsive page is resized. For information on responsive lanes, read the topic on "Responsive Lanes".

The Grid page is a space where you can create and work with formulas, either directly on the page or with a table that has been generated from existing data.

  • Design the page, Add Filter option & charts you want to display in the dashboard.

  •  Save the report with Report Name.

Running the Report

  • Go to  Report Center.

  • Click on Run button to execute the report.

Report Output:

Sharing the Report

  1. In Report Center, click on the “Share” menu of a Story you want to share and follow one of the three options available to share the report.

  1. Option 1: To share the report with users, click User.
    1. Enter the user name to whom you want to share the report.
    2. Use the additional fields: Department, Division, Location, and User Role to further filter the user selection.

Note: Don't use space or combination of first name, last name, partial name, full name as it doesn't return any result. For example, Adele Smoke doesn't return any result.

Option 2: To share the report with groups, click Group.

    1. Enter the group name to whom you want to share the report.
    2. Use the additional fields: Group Creator Username, Department, Division, Location, Last Modified Date Range, User Role, and Group Type to further filter the user selection.

Option 3: To share the report with the roles, click Role.

    1. Enter the role name to whom you want to share the report.
    2. Use the additional fields: Role Creator Name, Role Creation Date Range, Role Last Modified By:Last Modified Date Range, and Role Type to further filter the user selection.

Click Go.

Based on your search, users, groups, or roles are listed under Matched Result.

  • Select the users, groups, or roles, with whom you share the report.
  • Select the type of access rights you want to provide to the shared report, such as Read, Edit or Full Access, and then click Ok.

Note: If a Story is shared with Admin users, by default they have the permissions to perform any action on the story irrespective of the access level you select while sharing the story. The access levels apply only to non-admin users

The Saved Successfully message appears, which indicates your Story has been shared successfully.

 

Conclusion

Creating a People Analytics report story in SuccessFactors Learning is about turning data into actionable insights that drive decision-making and improve learning programs. By defining clear objectives, gathering and analyzing relevant data, crafting a compelling narrative, and effectively visualizing your findings, you can produce reports that not only inform but also inspire action. Embrace the power of analytics to enhance your learning strategies and contribute to your organization’s success.

Feel free to tailor this approach to your specific needs and the unique aspects of your SuccessFactors Learning environment. The goal is to make your data meaningful and impactful for your organization.

Note: All images are taken from the sales demo instance.

entry.title: Split Valuation in SAP MM
entry.content:
Introduction:

Split valuation is a feature in SAP Material Management (MM) that enables companies to manage and assign different values to various segments of the same material within the same valuation area (such as a plant or company code). This functionality is only applicable when using the moving average price control, meaning that materials subject to split valuation can only be valued using the moving average price method.

Reasons for Implementing Split Valuation:

  • Different Material Origins: When a material comes from different sources.
  • Variations in Quality: When the same material exists in different grades of quality.
  • Material Status Differences: When the material has different statuses.
  • In-house vs. External Procurement: Differentiating between materials produced internally and those procured from external suppliers.
  • Variation in Deliveries: Differentiating between different deliveries of the same material.

Valuation and Accounting Considerations:

Each valuation type within split valuation can have its own distinct valuation price. This allows for financial differentiation even if the material code remains the same across items. The valuation can vary based on factors such as the supplier or quality of the material, directly impacting accounting and costing processes. For instance, different batches can be assigned different prices, reflecting their unique characteristics.

Use Cases:

Split valuation is particularly beneficial in industries where the cost of materials is heavily influenced by procurement or production conditions. This is common in sectors such as pharmaceuticals, chemicals, or food and beverages, where different batches may originate from various sources or be produced under varying conditions, resulting in differing values.

Benefits of Split Valuation:

  • Enhanced Costing Accuracy: By assigning different values to stocks based on specific characteristics, companies can achieve more accurate costing and pricing strategies.
  • Improved Inventory Management: Businesses can better manage inventories with diverse origins, qualities, or other attributes, ensuring efficient and effective stock utilization.
  • Regulatory Compliance: In highly regulated industries, like pharmaceuticals, correctly tracking and valuing batches is crucial for compliance.
  • Risk Management: By categorizing stocks according to valuation types, companies can better manage risks related to price fluctuations, supplier reliability, or quality differences.

Split Valuation needs to be activated before setting any other configuration. Follow the steps given below to activate split valuation

Path to Activate Split Valuation

IMG ⇒ Materials Management ⇒ Valuation and Account Assignment ⇒ Split Valuation ⇒ Activate Split Valuation

TCode: OMW0

Step 1 − On the Display IMG screen, select Activate Split Valuation by following the above path.

Step 2 − Click the option Split Material Valuation Active. Click on Save. Split valuation is now activated.

Some important terms under split valuation are as follows.

  • Valuation Area − Material may be valuated at plant level or at company code level. The level at which materials are valuated is known as valuation area.
  • Valuation Category − Valuation category is the criteria on which material is split valuated.
  • Valuation Type − Valuation type specifies the features of a valuation category.

Split Valuation Configuration

Follow the steps given below to do split valuation configuration such as defining valuation category and valuation type.

Path to Configure Split Valuation

IMG ⇒ Materials Management ⇒ Valuation and Account Assignment ⇒ Split Valuation ⇒ Configure Split Valuation

T Code: OMWC

Step 1 − On the Display IMG screen, select Configure Split Valuation by following the above path.

Step 2 − Select Global Types to create valuation type.

Step 3 − Select the Create 

Step 4 − Provide the name of the valuation type. Click on Save. A new Valuation Type will be created.

Step 5 − Now go to the same screen and click Global Categories to create valuation category.

Step 6 − Select the Create 

Step 7 − Provide the name of the valuation category and Attributes. Select Save. A new Valuation Category will be created.

Step 8 – Select Valuation Category and Click the Types -->Cat.

Step 9 – Select respective Valuation Type for above selected Valuation Category and Select Activate.

Step 10 − Now go to the same screen and Select Local Definitions to map valuation type and valuation category.

Step 11 − Select the Cats. → OU 

Step 12 – select valuation category set the status as active, and then click the Activate. Valuation category and valuation area are now mapped and both are activated.

Material Creation with Split Valuation

1.    Create Material  (MM01)

Create material with required views.

Mention details in required field in all selected views

And in Accounting View 1, should specify the valuation category

Step 2: Create material with Valuation type

Specify Material Number and select only Accounting View 1

Mention the Valuation Type,

 

 

 

Material has created with valuation Type

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA integration with FSM setup activities
entry.content:
Introduction:

Integrating SAP Field Service Management (FSM) with SAP s/4HANA is crucial for optimizing field service operation and achieving seamless and data flow across systems. In this blog, we will go through the steps involved in setting up the SAP S4 HANA and FSM cloud for newly created company.

System requirements:

  • SAP S4 HANA On-Premise.
  • SAP FSM Cloud account.
  • SAP Cloud connector(YAML file / Message broker)

 

Steps involved:

  • E4C Connector setup in S4.
  • Configure SAP S4.
  • Create FSM company in cloud using E4C cloud manager.
  • Message broker configuration .
  • Configure FSM cloud.

 

E4C Connector setup in S4:

Integrating SAP S/4HANA with FSM using the E4C connector is essential for enabling seamless service management and operational efficiency. This section outlines the steps to configure and activate the E4C connector for this specific integration.

  1. Logical system:

A logical system is an application system in which the applications are coordinated to work in one common database. In SAP terms, a logical system corresponds to a client. The logical system is important in the following SAP areas- Communication between two or more logical systems.
We can create the logical system using below T-code : SALE > Basic Settings > Logical Systems > Define Logical systems.

Logical System 

Short Description 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

 

  1. Distribution model:

The Distribution Model defines the message type of data to be exchanged. The distribution model consists of separate model views in which all the associated, cross-system message flows in your organization can be defined. These views must be distributed to the receiving systems.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : SALE > Distribution Model > Edit > Create Model View.

Model 

Short Description 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

 

  1. Partner:

Partner profiles are a prerequisite for data exchange. This involves defining who can exchange messages with the SAP system and using which port. The partner profile contains parameters that define the electronic interchange of data with a partner via that IDoc interface.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : WE20.

Partner No 

Partner Type 

TY 

Agent (Technical User) 

Lang 

(System name)

  

(User_name) 

EN 

 

  1. Port:

The port defines the technical characteristics of the connection between SAP and the Subsystem. It also defines the medium in which data is exchanged between the two systems.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : WE21.

Port 

Short Description 

Function Module 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

/PACG/ECM_S_PORT  

 

  1. Configure web service:

A web service is any piece of software that makes itself available over the internet and uses a standardized XML messaging system. XML is used to encode all communications to a web service.

T-code: SM80 Go to Package - /PACG/ECM  

  • Service Consumer:  CLAS/PACG/CO_ECMISEND_OUTBOUND_MES

 

  • Go to SOA MANAGER 

Tcode: SOAMANAGER -> Web Service Configuration > Object Name: /PACG/CO_ECMISEND_OUTBOUND_MES 

  • Here we will be using the same logical Port we created earlier.

Logical Port Name 

Description 

Logical Port is default 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

/PACG/ECM_S_PORT  

  • Consumer Security Point :Maintain the Username and Password, this will be used in Message Broker.
  • Message Broker URL Components : Protocol, Path, Port, Host.
  • SOAP Protocol : Please choose the below while setting up the service in SOA Manager 

Message ID Protocol 

Data Transfer Scope 

Suppress ID Transfer 

Minimal Data Transfer 



 

Configuration in SAP S4:

Here we can see the detailed steps and T-codes required for setting up the objects in  SAP S/4HANA for newly created company.
we can use the FSM connector i,e /N/PACG/EC-> Customizing -> Objects. Or we can use the direct T-codes.

T-code

Object name

Object description

/n/pacg/ecm_eodef

General Properties of E4C 

The content of this table is generated by a setup program which defines all the objects available in FSM Cloud 

/n/pacg/ecm_cprop

ERP Object Definition 

Customize general, company-independent properties of FSM Cloud Connector.

/n/pacg/ecm_cmsgtype

External Message Type Definition 

This is used to get the assignment between external message  type and SAP Message type. This is a kind of the translation from the outside world to  the internal SAP common language 

/n/pacg/ecm_eoas

Object Assignment 

The content of this table is generated by the setup program provided by Proaxia. Definition of all E4C object types available in SAP.

/n/pacg/ecm_saasg

Sales Area Assignment 

Here you can define the cloud company against Sales Organization, Distribution channel and Division.

/n/pacg/ecm_plas

Plant Assignment 

We can define the plant that will be used in cloud for material consumption. Objects are sent from SAP to cloud companies. Cloud Companies are reflected in E4C Companies.

/n/pacg/ecm_paas

Personnel Area Assignment 

The personnel area is an organizational unit that represents a specific area of the enterprise and is organized according to aspects of personnel, time management and payroll

/n/pacg/ecm_clao

Allowed Checklist Objects 

The mapping between the E4C Object Type which can be used by the Checklists and the corresponding SAP Object Type. It is used to create a GOS-Link between the Checklist and the referenced object.

/n/pacg/ecm_cmsgon

Configuration of Online Calls 

Cloud message type – this external message type name is used to get the assignment between external message type and SAP message type

/n/pacg/ecm_comp

Company Definition 

Company can be defined as a business unit which can be used in the system as a primary note for all business operation

/n/pacg/ecm_caac

Company account assignment 

This links the cloud account to the company Id and logical port . Each company ID is connected to a i system and logical port at the platform level by the Basis team. The combination of logical system and logical port controls the data traffic from S4 system to the company ID. The data traffic also controls the movement of data from the FSM cloud to the technicians’ mobile device.

/n/pacg/ecm_acurc

Company code for allowed currencies 

Company for which currencies is restricted. This will be set as one company ID per currency that is allowed for that company

/n/pacg/ecm_acur

Allowed Currencies 

For a company ID the allowed currencies are maintained here.

/n/pacg/ecm_acou

Allowed Countries 

For a company ID the allowed countries are maintained here.

/n/pacg/ecm_ccust 

General Customizing for Customer 

The company ID is linked to customer standard and non-standard bill-to and Ship-to address

/n/pacg/ecm_ktokdmap

Customer Account Group Mapping 

Customer account group in each company is mapped to an external account group. A customer account group can be assigned to a number range with external or internal numbering as a default.

/n/pacg/ecm_eq1

Equipment Setting 

Here we enable the equipment and Functional locations (Telo is defined here as functional location) are transferred to the cloud. In FSM, Equipment data and function location data are transferred to the same master data table which is mapped to the equipment object. Whenever there are mater data needs to be imported from S4 to FSM, the equi&Telo settings are changed to “Equipment and Functional Location”.

/n/pacg/ecm_cinc01

Include Types of Equipment’s 

This includes types of equipment categories that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_cinc02

Include Types of Technical Location 

This includes types of Functional locations that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_eqsm

 Equipment Cloud Status Mapping 

 The includes the equipment and functional locations status that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_itype

 Item – Material Group and Company Assignment 

On material type level we can define the material type which needs to be transmitted to the cloud for each company ID

PACG/ECM_ICONF

Item - Item Configuration

 On item configuration we can define if the material should be reflected  to FSM as batch or Normal based on the check MBEW setup 

/n/pacg/ecm_ibs

Item – Batch or Serial numbers 

Item type to FSM to distinguish batch and serial materials 

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhquant

Item – Quantities to send to cloud 

Here we define which stock types needs to be sent to the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_iwlon

Item Warehouse Level – Good Movement Send Online 

You must define which Movement Types are relevant to be sent to the cloud. Movement Type 261 (GI for Order) is used to transfer Goods from Warehouse when the Technician consumes the Goods from FSM. A Material Document is created in S4 to record the Goods Movement.

/n/pacg/ecm_rmgm

Item Warehouse Level – Reserved Material: Action on GM 

If a Component is entered in a Service order a material reservation is created in E4C and sent to the cloud. This is a pre-warning that a shipment is outstanding.

/n/pacg/ecm_active

E4C Activity – General Activity Property 

The general details of an activity that belongs to a service call setup is done here.

/n/pacg/ecm_acti_type

E4C Activity – Activity Type 

Cloud Data Model offers certain Activity Types – for scheduling purposes the type “ASSIGNMENT” is in use. In SAP we define a counterpart.

/n/pacg/ecm_acti_statu

E4C Activity E4C Activity Status 

The ERP status and the Cloud status are mapped here.

/n/pacg/ecm_actmae

E4C Activity – SAP Activity Code Mapping 

Map internal & external activity type. Here reminder defines a general reminder notifying the user in mobile before activity start.

/n/pacg/ecm_extscpdef

Service call – External Priority Definition 

Definition of the values and description of the order priority in the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_screq

Service call – Service Call Sending – Requirements 

Definition of the status that must be achieved before sending the order to the cloud. We distinguish between Initial load (= Full sync) or the standard delta (changes only) transmission mode

/n/pacg/ecm_scstd

Service call – Status of Service Call 

Mapping of an SAP-System status of a Service order to the possible Cloud statuses. Mapping System status of a Service order in combination with priority, Status and description for the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_scg

Service call – Service Call General Settings 

If this is set here, then activities/operations are listed in the effort type for technician while working on the activity. To avoid them please do not check this option.

/n/pacg/ecm_sctd

Service Call – Service Call Type Definition 

Here we can set up the different service call type and its parameters that will be sent to cloud based on company

/n/pacg/ecm_sctdm

Service call – Movements for Service Call Type

The below table defines the Goods Movement types that are allowed to cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_scpriomap

Service Call – SAP Service Call Priority Mapping

Key that determines which priorities are allowed for a notification type.

/n/pacg/ecm_tkdef

Service Call – Task Control Key Activation

In this step, we maintain the allowed control keys for the individual operations of the maintenance and service orders.

/n/pacg/ecm_actgs

Service Call – Activity Feedback – [General settings] 

In this step, we configure feedback codes for three level for each company codes.

/n/pacg/ecm_sercd

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Code Groups]

In this step, we maintain the various code groups and levels of their appearance in FSM. These are required to update the service notification in S4.

/n/pacg/ecm_serfp

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Level Nr] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of levels to code groups. These are required to update the service notification in S4 to the correct code groups. 

/n/pacg/ecm_secma

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Activity Codes] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of codes to material(profile). This setting is required to filter codes based on profile related to Functional location in FSM.

/n/pacg/ecm_segma

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Activity Group] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of code groups to material(profile). This setting is required to filter code groups based on profile related to Functional location in FSM.

/n/pacg/ecm_sasa

Service Call – Assignment Status  

In this step, we maintain the user status with the operation workflow step of FSM. The workflow step is updated in operation user status of the service order.

/n/pacg/ecm_snot_gener

General properties of service notification

Define determination of plant for deciding a notification is sending relevant.

/n/pacg/ecm_snot_types

 Notification types 

Defines Mapping of Service Notification per company and type to identify Customer, Contact Person, Responsible Person and the source for long text. 

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhgen

Warehouse – General settings 

Definition of warehouses which are relevant for sending warehouse description to FSM

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhouse

 Warehouse - E4C Warehouse 

Definition of warehouses which are relevant for sending, master data, stock level and material movements

/n/pacg/ecm_ctet

 Work Time and Time Effort – Time Effort Type 

With this customizing you can define the different Work time effort types which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_canab

Work Time and Time Effort – CATS attendance or absence type

With this customizing you can define the different activity types associated with attendance/absence type which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_vwrktc

 Work Time and Time Effort – Work time Task Definition 

With this customizing you can define the different Work times and map to attendance/absence type which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_chop

 Work Time and Time Effort – Charge Options 

With this customizing you can define if the effort is of charge types which can be selected within the App and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction. The Description field is blank to allow notes from technician to be posted to CATS.

/n/pacg/ecm_udf_conf

reports UDF – UDF Definitions 

Workforce Management handles a User Defined Field (UDF) where the SAP FSM Cloud Connector will send commands. UDF (User Defined Fields) In SAP FSM, we can use UDF functionality to define Custom Fields. UDF fields can be created to fit scenarios and align closely with business needs. The UDF fields can store data in the cloud for automation, validation, dashboards and reporting purposes. 

/n/pacg/ecm_userg

Users – General Settings

This setting is established in the connector to identify which field of the User data is mapped with the Company Code. By setting this configuration admin will use the User data (ID) to send the data from S4 to FSM

/n/pacg/ecm_user

Users – Maintenance View

List of all users sent to FSM application will be maintained in this section. Only users’ data which are available in this table will be sent to FSM using Send programs. 

 

 

Create FSM company in cloud using E4C cloud manager:

Login to the cloud account in where in which system you would like to create the FSM company by providing the cloud account and its password.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Message broker configuration:

As per SAP architecture, Message broker is the middleware to connect SAP S4 and SAP FSM cloud system. Message broker is a hardware installed which is used  for installing the software component for the connector setup. 

This will be done by Basis team where they add newly created company name mapped with logical system and logical port and restart the MB service.

 

Configure FSM cloud:

  • Login in SAP FSM admin by filling in Account name, username, and password.

 

  • Navigate to Companies after logging in.

 

  • After the company is created, it will appear in companies’ section as shown in the below screenshot. 

     

 

  • Translations: The Translations tab is used to create and manage custom translations that can be used in different places in the application, such as in screen configurations, in business rule actions, or even in widgets for a given company. The Translations tab can be accessed by navigating to Admin > Company > Translations. Here we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

 

 

 

  • Company settings: Here we can customize company settings as per the requirements. Also, we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

 



  • Business rules: Go to the reference company and select Business Rules, Select the business rules that are required for the new company based on requirement and download it Upload the downloaded business rules in the newly created company.

 

  • Screen configuration: Here we can create custom rules as per the requirements . These can be Downloaded and uploaded to any other existing companies as required. Also, initially we can find set of already defines business rules by SAP.

 



  • Service workflow: The steps which we define here will be available in FSM Mobile Application. The Service Workflow feature can be reached from Admin > Company > Service Workflow. All users with access to the admin app can access this feature.

 

Once you clicked on create, you will find the options to add a name to your service workflow, to make that workflow as default and you can provide name, description & indicate next step for the steps which you add. By clicking the eye icon beside the step, you can edit the data for that step.

 

  • Custom objects: Go to the reference company and select Custom objects->Custom field definitions, you can create a custom object manually here by clicking on the create.

Note: All the other Custom field definitions would be configured and sent from S4(UDF) to FSM, we can create manually only if its required.

 

  • Checklist: Go to Shell Application and click on Smartforms and feedback for the reference company.

 

Click on Templates and all the created checklists would appear.

 

From Reference company, Double clicks on the checklist and from the 3 dots, click on export XML, which will download the checklist.

and click on the + sign in the bottom right corner, where you will be getting an option to add the category name upon clicking on ok, new category will be created. And on your right side under the import templates section, you can upload the downloaded file for your newly created category. By double clicking on the file name inside category you will be redirected to new page where you can activate the checklist by releasing it.

Note- For the new company, this checklist would not be appearing and would be empty as this is a reference image from the reference company.

  • Maintain the Checklist: A After releasing the checklist in Shell, go to tcode - se16- /PACG/ECM_CLTTH and give company ID and execute it.

 

 

In another tab, go to tcode - sm30 – Custom checklist table tcode and maintain it.

 

Go to the new company and edit all the checklist values from another tab i.e.  - se16- Custom checklist table tcode.

entry.title: #2- Practical guide: 4 steps to reversing and recreating documents in TD-TM integration
entry.content:
By: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcsousap/

After configuring the integration between SAP TD and SAP TM, as described in the blog post below:

  • How I configured the integration between SAP TD and SAP TM modules in 8 steps

I ran some tests and had trouble reversing the documents created automatically during the integration. I therefore thought it would be interesting to document the way I found to reverse, recreate and display the documents.

This guide is based on the “Status TM” tab of the transportation document (app o4f3), one of the screens that shows all the documents created in the integration.

1. Access the Transport document - TM Status tab

  • Attention: This step is important in order to see and note down the document numbers that have been created in SAP TM.

2. Cancel Freight Order

Before

After

3. Cancel Freight Unit

Antes

Depois

  • Attention: During testing, I realized that when I reversed the Freight Order, I also had to reverse the Freight Unit. Only then was it possible to carry out the next step successfully.”

4. Quantity assignment

a. Access the APP o4l1, enter the transport document number and click on Create/Modify.

b. On the screen that appears, click on the “Yes” button

c. On the screen that appears, click on the “Save” button

d. Checking documents recreated in the o4f3 app

Points to note when reversing documents

During testing, I noticed that when reversing any of the documents in the TM (FU or FO), the SAP eliminates the document flow that appears in the o4f3 app, as shown in the example below:

  • Document flow between SAP TD and SAP TM modules that appears in the o4f3 App

  • Cancel Freight Order

When you cancel the Freight Order, SAP still maintains the link between the freight unit (TM) and the shipment document (TD).
You can see this via the Freight unit's document flow tab, as shown in the image below:

  • Freight Unit 4100000260 - Document flow tab

Meanwhile, in the TM Status tab of the o4f3 App, there are no TM documents.

After a series of tests, I realized that in order to successfully reverse the documents, I had to follow the 4 steps described in this blog post.

entry.title: Solution for EWM queue failure “There are no delivery documents for these selection criteria”
entry.content:
SUBJECT:

Steps to replicate and resolve the queue failure with error message “There are no delivery documents for these selection criteria”

Stock Transfer Order:

A Stock Transfer Order (STO) is a document or request used in inventory management and logistics to transfer goods or stock from one location to another within the same organization or between different branches or warehouses. It helps manage and track the movement of inventory to ensure that the right quantities are available at the right locations when needed.

Inbound delivery:

An inbound delivery is a logistics term referring to the process and documentation involved in receiving goods from suppliers or vendors into a warehouse or distribution center. It is a critical component of the supply chain, focusing on the receipt of inventory and its subsequent integration into the warehouse management system.

Outbound delivery:

An outbound delivery refers to the process and documentation involved in dispatching goods from a warehouse or distribution center to customers, other warehouses, or locations within the supply chain. It encompasses all activities related to preparing, packing, and shipping items out of a facility.

Reference Document Categories:

Reference Document Categories are classifications used within information management and enterprise systems, such as ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) and document management systems, to organize and categorize various types of documents and records. These categories help streamline document management, retrieval, and processing by providing a structured way to classify and manage different types of documents related to business processes.

ASN:

Advance Shipping Notification, is a crucial document in the logistics and supply chain management processes. It is sent by a supplier or shipper to inform the recipient (such as a warehouse or customer) of the upcoming shipment and provide details about the contents and expected arrival.

BOL:

Bill of Lading (BOL) is a crucial document in the transportation and shipping industry that serves multiple purposes, including acting as a contract of carriage, a receipt of goods, and a document of title. It is used to facilitate the shipping process and ensure that goods are transported from the sender to the recipient as agreed.

Steps to replicate and resolve the queue failure with error message “There are no delivery documents for these selection criteria”

The Reference document categories configuration has the ASN and BOL inactive options check marked.

 

Scenario:

Stock transfer order is created using the Transaction code ME21N

Advance return delivery created using transaction code VL31N

 

Complete Picking and Goods Issue in S4

Inbound delivery created Automatically in S4

The external id populates Automatically with Outbound delivery number.

 

Completed Goods receipt and Putaway against IBD in EWM

 

The external id changed to empty with no Outbound delivery number and therefore the queue DLWSDEPCLNT1200400057211 is stuck with the error message “There are no delivery documents for these selection criteria.”

 

After configuration change by unchecking Inactive for ASN and BOL:

 

I have reset the queue, but the error persists.

 

 

 
Result: Reprocessing the queue does not work as the entry in LIKP-LIFEX is still missing and the absence of outbound delivery in the external id field of the inbound delivery.

For the queue to be successfully reprocessed the field LIFEX (External Field) of the Inbound delivery should be filled with Outbound delivery number in LIKP table but whereas the new deliveries created after the above configuration maintained will no longer face the error. 


Retested Scenario after the configuration is changed:

Stock transfer order created using the transaction code ME21N

Return delivery created using the transaction code VL31N 

Inbound delivery created Automatically in S4

 

The external id populates Automatically with Outbound delivery number.

Completed Goods Receipt and Put away against IBD in EWM

 

 

The external id still populates Outbound delivery number after Goods Receipt completed.

 

 

 

No queue error occurred.

The scenario is processed successfully.

Conclusion:

Steps to replicate and resolve the queue failure with error message “There are no delivery documents for these selection criteria” is provided.

entry.title: SAP Advanced ATP functionalities - To maximize the order confirmation
entry.content:
The objective of this blog is to showcase how the different Advanced ATP (aATP) functionalities put together can make a difference in final-order confirmation scenarios. The SAP Standard functionalities can help us to achieve that objective.

Problem Statement: ABC Corporation is in the consumer goods industry, and they identified specific issues for their order confirmation process in real-time. They want to use Advanced ATP functionalities and want to leverage the maximum of it. The following issues are identified:

  1. Unused stocks are lying at different plant levels without proper information for the business.
  2. If customers accept, they want to use the closest substitute products to fulfils the customer demand.
  3. They want to put some restrictions on the order confirmation so that profitability and customer satisfaction can be maintained for premium customers.
  4. At the end of the day, Backorder processing should be triggered as per business rules so that reprioritization of demands can happen accordingly.

Business Scenario:  The business got an order of 800 pcs of Material 1 (TG14). And they want to do maximum Order confirmation based on Available stock in hand and closet substitute material.

Considering the above problem statement, the business wants to leverage the standard Advanced ATP functionalities. They can use different functionalities in one go and maximize the order fulfillment rate in real time. We will explore the same further:

Step No.aATP FunctionalityScenario DescriptionMaterialQty ( In pcs.)
1Product Availability Check (PAC)Available Qty in Plant 1 (Plant 1710)
Demand received from Customer		TG14661
800
2ABC-Location SubstitutionTo increase the Order confirmation qty by
 switching to another location where Material is available.
Available Qty in Plant 2 (Plant 1712) 		TG1410
3ABC-Product SubstitutionTo increase the Order confirmation qty by
Substitution to closely related material in Plant 1 (1710).		MZ-FG-C90140
4Supply Protection (SuP)Substitute material has a restriction on order confirmation,
Supply Protection (SuP) is activated for the substitute Material.		MZ-FG-C90112
5 &6 Analysis of Sales Order Confirmation scenario before BOP run.TG 14 &MZ-FG-C901 
7Backorder Processing (BOP)Backorder processing runs at the end of the day and reprioritizes the Sales OrdersTG 14 & MZ-FG-C901 

Pre-requisites:

  1. Classic ATP-related system configuration in place.
  2. Advanced ATP is activated in the system.
  3. FIORI configurations for ABC Product & Location Substitution, Supply Protection (SuP), and Backorder Processing (BOP) are already in place.
  4. The Master data set up is in place.

We are not going to discuss each functionality of Advanced ATP and FIORI configuration setup in this blog rather we will focus on a single scenario, how we can utilize the relevant Advanced ATP features and maximize the order confirmation within the scope SAP standard functionalities.

Proposed Solution: These are the step-by-step process for the proposed solutions.

Step 1: Demand generated for 800 pcs for the Customer. Product Availability Check triggered and confirmed the Available stock of 661 pcs from Plant 1 (1710). We are utilizing the Product Availability Check (PAC) functionality.

Step 2: To fulfill the demand, the business wants to confirm the order qty from another plant i.e. Plant 2 (1712) as the Sales order Plant i.e. Plant 1 (1710) doesn’t have enough available stock in hand.

Relevant aATP FIORI configuration:  

We have leveraged ABC Location substitution functionalities, and the following are the details: ABC Substitution Strategy:

ABC Location Substitution Master data set-up:

Here Plant 1 (1710) is getting substituted with Plant 2 (1712).

Step 3: To further increase the order confirmation the business wants to substitute the closest feature-wise product and confirmation happens according to the same.

Relevant aATP Master data setup: ABC Product Substitution:

Master data set up for material TG14. 

Step 4: The alternative substituted product is in high demand; the business wants to put restrictions based on certain parameters. As this order does not fulfill those parameters, order confirmation is restricted to the same.

Here the available qty is 52 and Supply Protection is activated for 12 pcs hence 52-12= 40 pcs got the confirmation.

Relevant aATP FIORI configuration:  The company is facing a more demand-less supply scenario for the substituted material (MZ-FG-C901). Business wants to put a restriction on the same. The proposed solution is the Supply Protection (SuP) functionality.

Here, the business wants to restrict the confirmation based on ‘Delivery Priority’. They want to use standard delivery priority as 01 and put the protected qty as 12. The said material has an available qty (Co09) of 52.

Step 5: In summary, how the confirmation happens, let’s analyze the same.

Step 6: How does the final confirmation look at the schedule line level, let’s analyze the same:

Material No.

Plant

Confirmed Qty

Impacted aATP functionality

TG 14

Plant 1

661

The customer placed the Order for TG14. And Available stock in hand

TG 14

Plant 2

10

ABC Location Substitution triggers to consume the stock from different locations.

MZ-FG-C901

Plant 1

40

ABC Product Substitution + Supply Protection works together to increase the order fulfillment rate.

Total Confirmation

 

711

 

 

 

Step 7: Backorder Processing run happens at the end of the day and reprioritization takes place as per the pre-defined rules.

Here, the business wants to prioritize based on ‘Delivery Priority’ and we have 2 sales orders ( SO) i.e. 6343 and 6344. The first Sales Order ( 6343) has a delivery priority of 02 ( Normal) and the 2nd Sales Order (6344) has a custom delivery priority of 10. Business wants to prioritize Delivery Priority 10 as the top priority during the prioritization of Order for BOP Run.

Custom sequence assigned to BOP segment:

BOP run result:

 

Here, in Sales Order 6344 the order confirmation changed from 0 to 10 and 6343 Order confirmation also revised from 711 to 701.

Let’s analze the Sales Order 6343 post-BOP Run.

 

Conclusion:

 We have now covered the scenario and got the expected outcome with respect to our problem statement. We achieved these results based on the SAP standard FIORI configuration for Advanced ATP (aATP).


Please share your questions, comments, and feedback.

Additional information on SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) can be found here:

Reference:

  1. Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) | SAP Help Portal
  2. 2776851 - FAQ: Alternative based Confirmation (ABC) with aATP | SAP Knowledge Base Article
  3. 3141787 - FAQ - Supply Protection (SUP) with advanced ATP

entry.title: Leveraging BRF+ Decision Tables for Flexible Route Determination in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
Use Case-

Discover how to streamline your sales order and stock transfer order (STO) processes in SAP S4HANA by replacing standard route determination with powerful BRF+ decision tables. This innovative approach empowers your business to integrate seamlessly with Excel, enabling efficient import and export of route data without IT intervention. Learn how to enhance flexibility, reduce manual effort, and gain valuable insights into your supply chain operations.

Perquisites:

  • Basic knowledge on ABAP concepts
  • Basic knowledge on BRF+ framework in S4 HANA 1709 and above
  • Knowledge on Route determination rules

What is BRF+

BRF+ is an ABAP based framework and part of the NetWeaver stack. BRF+ stands for Business Rule Framework Plus and it provides a comprehensive application programming interface and user interface for defining business rules. It enables you to define business rules without the need of wiring ABAP code. The generated Business Rules can be incorporated into other SAP programs or Substitution/Validation rules.

Services for BRF+ Activation in S4 HANA

In order to use BRF+ the following services need to be activated through transaction SICF:

  • /SAP/BC/WEBDYNPRO/SAP/FDT_WD_WORKBENCH

  • /SAP/BC/WEBDYNPRO/SAP/FDT_WD_OBJECT_MANAGER

 

  • –/SAP/BC/WEBDYNPRO/SAP/FDT_WD_CATALOG_BROWSER

You can launch BRF+ UI afterwards by entering t-code BRF+ or BRFplus. Further technical and functional detail about BRF+ is available at SAP help via this link.

Functional Configuration using BRF+

The steps in the BRF+ UI will consist of the follow steps:

  1. Application creation- Create an Application using t-code BRF+.
  2. Data Object Creation- Data Object Creation consists of all the variables used , which will be used for processing. These variables are basically input data to the BRF+ to process and to generate the output/result. In this use Case- We need to add relevant for Route determination- like Shipping point(TVST/Shipping condition(VSBED)/Country(LAND1)/Route(ROUTE)- Our Decision.
  3. Function Creation- Next step , is to create Functions which is the core component - where the actual processing of the functionality gets executed.
  4. Ruleset Creation- Inside the Functions we have a Rule Set , which is basically a container which holds all Set of Rules and these Rules are processed based on the decision tables.
  5. Decision Table Creation- Decision tables are going to hold the data which consists of all possible Inputs & corresponding Output values- in this case our Decision is always “ROUTE”. Basically , the Data Objects import parameters validate against the Decision Tables and these Decision table will generate the outcome/result.

  • Simulation of the Business Rule- It is possible and recommended to simulate the Rule Set to see if your function is working fine. Go to the Function and navigate to the simulation screen

       

  • Now the Question is?- how to call this decision tables  in SAP Sales order creation in place of Standard Route Determination.?In the function component . you have an option called Create Code Template - after selecting this , it shows the generated code template. The generated code is the one which you need to place in the  EXIT_SAPL0VRF_001, Include ZXV00U01- Customer specific Route determination  

Enhanced Efficiency and Flexibility

  • Direct Data Access: View and modify decision table data directly from your preferred environment (development,/quality, or production) without requiring BRF+ API logins.
  • Simplified : Streamline the process of creating and moving transport requests for new rule updates, saving time and effort.
  • Version Comparison: Gain a clear understanding of rule changes with the version comparison feature, facilitating effective tracking and analysis.
  • Granular Access Control: Implement robust security measures by defining specific authority checks and roles to control access to BRF+ decision tables.
  • Comprehensive Change History: Maintain a detailed record of rule modifications with the change log history feature, providing valuable reference

Looking to implement customer-specific route determination in SAP S4HANA without extensive coding? This use case demonstrates how BRF+ decision tables can provide a flexible and efficient solution. By leveraging BRF+, you can easily configure and manage route determination rules, enabling greater customization  and integration with external systems like Excel. Share your thoughts and potential use cases for BRF+ in the comments below!

entry.title: Solution for Queue Error "Product 9122 serial no. requirement; qty role SN missing for item type"
entry.content:
Stock Transfer Order (STO):

A Stock Transfer Order (STO) is a document used in supply chain management and inventory control to facilitate the movement of stock from one location to another within the same organization. This is often necessary for balancing inventory levels across different warehouses or branches. 

Inter Company STO:

An Inter-Company Stock Transfer Order (STO) is a specific type of Stock Transfer Order that occurs between different legal entities or subsidiaries within the same parent company or corporate group. This type of transfer involves the movement of goods between entities that are part of the same corporate structure but operate as separate, distinct companies. 

Inbound delivery:

An Inbound Delivery is a logistics and supply chain term that refers to the process of receiving goods into a warehouse or storage facility. It involves various steps to ensure that products are correctly received, inspected, and recorded into the inventory system. 

Assignment of Quantity role:

The Assignment of Quantity role in inventory management and logistics refers to the process of allocating or assigning specific quantities of inventory to different roles, tasks, or purposes within a business. This is a crucial function in managing inventory effectively, ensuring that stock is available where it’s needed, and optimizing supply chain operations.

Quantity offsetting profile:

A Quantity Offsetting Profile is a concept often used in inventory management, accounting, or supply chain systems to handle the reconciliation of discrepancies or adjustments in quantities. It’s designed to manage how inventory or financial discrepancies are addressed and offset within a system. 

Quantity role SN:

The Quantity Role SN is a concept used in inventory management, particularly within systems that track serial numbers and manage inventory with serial number tracking. The "SN" stands for "Serial Number," which is a unique identifier assigned to individual items or units of inventory.

Steps to replicate and solution for Queue Error "Product 9122 serial no. requirement; qty role SN missing for item type"

Step1: Created Stock transfer order using transaction code ME21N

 

Step2: Create Outbound delivery using transaction code VL10B

 

Since its EWM managed the delivery is distributed.

 

Step3: Queue stuck in SMQ2 with error message as “Product serial no. requirement; qty role SN missing for item type” 500072144

 

This is caused because of the Assignment of quantity role SN is inactive in the configuration.

 

 Step4: Current configuration maintained

The Assignment of Quantity Roles SN is maintained as inactive.

 

Step5: Changes in Configuration

The Assignment of Quantity Roles SN is not maintained as inactive.

Step6: Go to Inbound queue using the transaction code SMQ2 and Reprocess the failed Queue.

 

 

 

Step7: Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM, Display using the transaction code /N/SCWM/PRDO

Conclusion:

Solution for the Queue Failure error message “Product 9122 serial no. requirement; qty role SN missing for item type” is provided.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Guide for EFT Terminal Integration with SAP Customer Checkout via Pepper
entry.content:
Dear implementation partners and clients: This guide provides an overview of common issues encountered during the integration of EFT terminals with SAP Customer Checkout using the Pepper Plugin by Treibauf. It highlights the most frequent error codes and offers troubleshooting steps to resolve them. Please note that this is not an exhaustive list of all possible error codes. For a comprehensive list of all error codes and detailed documentation, please visit the following link: https://eftdriver.com/process.php?do=download&id=2881. (Note: You must log in to Treibauf to access this file).

Most of the error codes are typically displayed in CCO during initialization or during a transaction. However, in some cases, they may only appear in the log. By default, the log is located under C:/Pepper/log. If these setups are not properly configured, the Pepper log file will instead be located in the SAP Customer Checkout directory.

 

Error Code: -102

Issue: Failure to load the pepcore library

Troubleshooting: Verify that the path to the pepcore library is correct and that the library file is present in the specified location.

Error Codes: -200 to -204

Issue: Configuration Errors

These error codes are related to issues with the configuration file or environment settings.

      • Error Code: -200: Generic Configuration File Error
      • Description: A general error in the configuration file that isn't tied to a specific issue.
      • Troubleshooting: Review the configuration file for inconsistencies or missing entries and compare it against the Pepper Developer Documentation.
      • Error Code: -201: Missing Environment Variable
      • Description: A required environment variable, such as PEPPER_CONFIGFILE, is missing or incorrectly set.
      • Troubleshooting: Verify that the environment variables are correctly configured according to the Pepper Developer Documentation.
      • Error Code: -202: Missing Configuration File
      • Description: The configuration file pepper_config.xml is missing or cannot be found in the expected directory.
      • Troubleshooting: Ensure that the configuration file is present in the correct directory and that the file path is accurately specified.
      • Error Code: -203: Invalid XML Structure
      • Description: The configuration file contains invalid XML syntax, such as mismatched tags or improper structure.
      • Troubleshooting: Use an XML validator to check for syntax errors and correct any invalid XML tags or formatting issues.
      • Error Code: -204: Invalid Pathname
      • Description: The configuration file contains an incorrect or invalid path.
      • Troubleshooting: Verify that all paths specified in the configuration file are correct and point to existing, accessible directories.

Error Codes: -300 to -302

Issue: Logging Initialization Failures

Description: These errors are related to failures in initializing the logging system. They often occur due to misconfigurations in the Pepper configuration file, particularly under the <Logging> and <Archive> tags.

      • Error Code: -300: Logging Generic Failure
      • Description: This is a logging initialization failure that does not fall under the specific errors -301 or -302.
      • Troubleshooting: Refer to the developer documentation for more details on the correct syntax and structure of the configuration file, especially for the <Logging> tag.
      • Error Code: -301: Logging Path Does Not Exist
      • Description: A logging initialization failure due to an invalid or non-existing directory specified in the Pepper configuration file.
      • Troubleshooting: Ensure that the directory specified in the Pepper configuration file exists and is accessible.
      • Error Code: -302: Logging Archive Creation Failure
      • Description: This failure occurs when there is an invalid entry in the <Archive> tag of the Pepper configuration file. This could result from a non-existing directory or an out-of-range value for the MaxAgeInDays tag.
      • Troubleshooting: Check the Pepper configuration file to ensure that the directory specified in the <Archive> tag exists and that the MaxAgeInDays value is within the valid range.

Error Codes: -800 to -805

Issue: License Library Failures

License-related failures due to invalid data, incorrect keys, or configuration settings.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Log Review: Analyze the Pepper log file to identify specific license-related errors.
      2. Verify License Data: Ensure that the license data in the configuration matches the expected version.
      3. Confirm License Key: Check for typographical errors or inconsistencies in the license key.
      4. Validate Terminal Type: Ensure that the terminal type specified is correct and supported by the license.
      5. Check License Validity: Confirm that the license is still valid and has not expired.
      6. Check License Path in Configuration File: Verify that the correct path for the license is configured in the Pepper configuration file.
      7. Check if License Path Exists: Ensure that the directory path specified in the configuration file exists and is accessible

Error Codes: -1400 to -1402

Issue: Business Logic Failures

These errors are triggered when business logic checks fail during transaction processing due to missing or incorrect values in the transaction data.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Verify Mandatory Fields: Ensure that all required fields (e.g., reference numbers, transaction IDs) are correctly populated in the Plugin Configuration.
      2. Check Values Additional Settings: Review the configuration for missing or incorrect values such as iConfigByteValue or sMerchantPasswordString (6 digits mandatory).

Error Codes: -2100 to -2106

Issue: Communication Failures

These error codes relate to communication problems between the EFT terminal and the system, ranging from general errors to specific failures in protocol management and resource initialization.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Check Terminal Reachability: Use Telnet to ensure the terminal is reachable and verify the port is open
      2. Check for IP Address Typos: Review the IP address configuration in the Pepper PLugin configuration for any typos.
      3. Ensure EFT is on Correct Network: Ensure the EFT terminal is on the correct network and turned on.
      4. Verify Firewall and Antivirus Settings: Ensure no firewall or antivirus software is blocking communication.
      5. Review Protocol Configurations: Verify that the communication protocol is correctly configured.
      6. Resource Initialization: Confirm all resources (network sockets, language resources) are correctly initialized during pepInitialize() and freed during pepFinalize().
      7. Tim OPI Server Configuration: If using Tim OPI, check that the server is correctly initialized and configured by the payment provider.

Error: Abortion of Specific Payment Types (e.g., Apple Pay)

Issue: Aborted Payment Types

Certain payment types, such as Apple Pay, may be aborted without displaying a specific error.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Check for Latest Pepper Update: Ensure that you are using the latest version of Pepper, which may include fixes for specific payment types.
      2. Review Logs: Check system logs for indications of why the payment was aborted.
      3. Communication Review: Verify that the communication configuration supports the payment type and that no issues are preventing it from being processed.

Error: Not Initialized

Issue: Plugin Initialization Failure

This occurs when a plugin fails to initialize, leading to issues with certain functionalities.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Fallback to Smaller Numbers: Use smaller numbers as fallback configurations if the system fails to initialize the payment method properly.

Error: Can't Save Values in CCO-Plugin Configuration Window (Technical Error)

Issue: Technical Error in CCO Plugin Configuration

Users are unable to save values in the SAP Customer Checkout plugin configuration window due to a technical error.

      • Troubleshooting Steps:
      1. Change Fallback Value: Modify the fallback value in the configuration to ensure compatibility with the plugin and correct functionality.
      2. Fill all Mandatory Values: Ensure all mandatory fields are filled with values

Error Code: -1

Issue: Generic Communication Error

A generic error occurs when the communication channel is destroyed, and no specific error message is logged.

    • Troubleshooting Steps:
    1. Check License Compatibility: Ensure that the license is compatible with the Pepper version (e.g., Pepper 1 license won't work with Pepper 2).
    2. Check Library and Compatibility: Verify that all library files and mandatory Pepper files are correctly placed and are compatible with the Pepper plugin version. Additionally, check if the Java version is compatible with the plugin.

entry.title: Re: Changes to Contracts with Advance Payment
entry.content:
Hi,

The provided ODI steps are used for BIT reversal and BIT re-request during contract change. During any contract change on active subscription contracts, ODI steps of P1 category are determined while during cancellation ODI steps of P6 category are used. Hence, there are two such ODI steps each for reversal and re-request.

To facilitate successful contract changes when BITs exist after the activation date, BIT reversal should be executed first followed by contract activation (or deactivation if cancellation is executed) and then BIT re-request. Hence, if you are using standard ODI steps, PBVC should be prerequisite step for PCEA and PCEA should be prerequisite step for PBRC.

Chapter 9.12.6 Contract Changes After Advance Payments of the integration guide 53c4442b1b0c4af18d8bbaf24caca345.pdf (sap.com) explains this. Thanks.

Regards
Deepak

entry.title: Code, Create, Celebrate: Go Digital Night at SAP Headquarters
entry.content:
After a long hiatus since 2018, the Go Digital Night a learning celebration made a grand return!

On a lovely Friday afternoon on August 2nd, the SAP Headquarters in Walldorf hosted an amazing learning party! More than 250 tech enthusiasts aged 12 and up, as well as educators, gathered for this special event, and the air was filled with curiosity and passion for continuous learning. During the event, we had international scientists, educators, and SAP teams leading workshops on various topics, from programming a microcontroller to understanding artificial intelligence or shaping an own sustainable business model or exploring SAP business software such as Success Factors and SAP Business Technology Platform. There were also creative activities, such as using a sewing machine or working with mandalas. There was something for everyone!

As the event kicked off, with an inspirational introduction by Christiane Bauer and Jadga Huegle everyone dived headfirst into a world of creativity and innovation. This scene was followed with an amazing warm-up lead by Matthias Haase from the Wissensfabrik giving a teaser to the participants about how software works.

Caption: a. Introduction by Christiane Bauer and Jadga Huegle | b. Matthias Haase during the warm-up activity | c. Introduction to SAP and founders | d. Participation of our guests | e. Introducing SAP customers using the example of a chocolate brand customer.

The theme "Code, create, celebrate" set the tone for the evening, guiding us through a series of awesome workshops led by enthusiastic experts.

  • Snap!GPT: In this workshop, Jens Mönig showed us the functionality and intelligence of AI language models using Snap!, together we programmed an algorithm for generating stories, and examined how different media influenced artificial creativity.
  • Pixel Art: In this workshop, Michael Winckler from IWR University of Heidelberg, showed participants how images were created from numbers. We didn't even need a computer because the translation of images into numbers, called "coding," could also be understood without electronics.
  • Mandalas Ojo de Dios: In this workshop, Jana Podobnik an enthusiastic SAP UX designer, created with us our own mandalas from wool. Experiencing in this way the cultural and spiritual significance of the "Ojo de Dios" tradition and promoted mindfulness and creative expression in the middle of digital atmosphere.
  • Art with Snap!: In this workshop, Gerd, Volker and Kitty Luise from “Mit Rat und Tat” presented us how to create fascinating artwork using the Snap! programming language through creative use of movement, color, and randomness, with the option to experience this in teams of two.
  • Robotics: In this workshop, Andreas Huppert, introduce us on the challenge of building and programing robots like Lego EV3, Boost, and mBot without prior knowledge.
  • Sustainable Business Model: In this workshop, our friends from WÖRK.APP taught us about how to use the Business Model Canvas method to develop and validate an startup idea from different perspectives in small teams.
  • BTP Diamond Game: In the SAP BTP Diamond Game, Yuri Kupriianov invited older participants like parents to playfully experience the SAP Business Technology Platform by making strategic decisions as executives and overcoming business challenges to gain practical insights into the application of SAP BTP.
  • MicroBlocks: In this workshop, Peter Mathijssen showed us how to control our computer remotely as if with a remote control. We programmed a microcontroller in MicroBlocks that interacted with our Snap! game or artwork. Through this, we discovered the world of sensors and what was possible with them.
  • Turtlestitch: In this workshop, Jadga Hügle and the TurtleStitch Gang (educators who joined this event after attending Snap!Shot 2024) programmed patterns with Turtlestitch and transferred them to the real world using a sewing machine or a cutting plotter. It was amazing! We even sewed a small Alonzo, the sweet character of Snap!.

In summary, we got to explore everything from programming languages like Snap! to virtual reality, coding, AI, robotics, and even how to develop sustainable business ideas. Not only our young audience was busy learning but also engaged parents participated in workshops designed for adults. The learning opportunities were practically endless!

Event highlights, our workshops and collaborators

But it wasn't just about the workshops – the sense of community and teamwork was just as exciting. We found ourselves sharing knowledge, collaborating, and cheering each other on. The SAP University Alliance team wants to extend our biggest thanks to our amazing collaborators:

  • SAP Vocational Training: Together with the vocational training team we learned directly from the source about the training and study opportunities at SAP.
  • SAP Experience Garage: At this stand, visitors had the opportunity to experience augmented reality in both "old" and "new" forms through two different examples, showcasing the advancements and changes in technology over time.
  • Virtual Reality with SAP Success Factors: Visitors had the opportunity to immerse themselves in virtual worlds and explore how VR technology is utilized in companies, offering a glimpse into the future of digital innovation.
  • Core Game with School 42 | Heilbronn: Here, School 42 a tuition-free, non-profit and innovative coding school offered a unique approach to education. The International Bot Game Coding Competition provided programmers of all levels with the opportunity to enhance their skills, take on challenges, and engage in discussions with fellow enthusiasts.
  • Wissensfabrik: At this stand, participants were able to independently test the IT2school AI materials and learn through unplugged examples how machines could become artificially intelligent, such as classifying monkeys or defeating individuals in farmers' chess.
  • TUMO Mannheim: TUMO Mannheim, a creative learning center that provides practical workshops in technology, design, and media for young people to develop their creative and technical skills in an interactive setting, also was present and showed our offers and resources with the audience.

Thanks, big time also to our SAP Next- Gen 4 ALL ambassadors, Interdisciplinary Center for Scientific Computing “IWR” (University of Heidelberg), University of Berkeley, University of Warwick and our external partners. Your contribution made this event truly special, impactful and unforgettable.

The event was filled with dynamic energy and provided a rich learning experience, as captured in these heartfelt interview highlights.

  • "I think it was very nice how the (Go Digital Night) was designed. It was very appealing for both children and adults." – Cecile, young learner
  • "I liked that each stand had several people and that thee workshops were very individual as a result" - Annika and Amishi, 13 & 14
  • "I really enjoyed it. It was a fun experience, and I learned something new." - Andi, young learner.
  • "The Virtual Reality workshop was really great, the (technology) is just incredible!" - Max & Mika, 14 & 13
  •  "I learned something new and beautiful (Mandalas Workshop)!"

     

  •  "I think that the (Go Digital Night) was great! You could decide for yourself what you wanted to learn, and I learned a lot of new things today!" 

As the evening progressed, we took a break to refuel with some delicious pizza and recharge our energy for the rest of the event. The lively atmosphere continued with a spontaneous dance party, complete with colorful balloons bouncing around the room. It was a joyful interlude that added an extra layer of fun to an already incredible evening!

As the night came to an end, we left with hearts full of inspiration, looking forward to the next exciting chapter of Go Digital Night. It was an unforgettable night, filled with laughter, learning, and the joy of discovery!

Thank you to all the supporters and organizers, we would love to celebrate more learning next year.

Caption: Our SAP team at the end of the event  

Key resources:

If you would like to take part next time, please check our SAP University Alliances and SAP Young Thinkers offering on our website.

  • SAP Young Thinkers | Who we are: link 
  • SAP University Alliances | Who we are: link
  • SAP Young Thinkers | SAP Community: link
  • Student opportunities at SAP: check it out here!

If you are already in the mood of getting started on your own dive into our online learning offerings here.

  • Student Zone: Curated learning content for students
  • Snap!: Fun programming language.

Do not hesitate to reach out for any questions or to engage via youngthinkers@sap.com.
